steps.title: Find the quantity clue
steps.content: The phrase “over one hundred that they have spotted” shows the team found many satellites, while others found only one or two.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the clue
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean numerous or abundant — Sheppard's team found over one hundred satellites while most astronomers find only one or two. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Inferior: discoveries have been "inferior" — inferior means lower in quality or rank, but the passage contrasts quantity (100+) with quantity (1–2), not quality. ✗
  • Choice B — Random: discoveries have been "random" — random means without pattern or purpose, which says nothing about how many were found. ✗
  • Choice C — Plentiful: discoveries have been "plentiful" — plentiful means existing in large amounts, which fits over one hundred discoveries compared to others' one or two. ✓
  • Choice D — Likely: discoveries have been "likely" — likely means probable, which describes expectation rather than actual quantity. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
